summary: react-simple-utils-kit ships an undisclosed postinstall SSRF/recon payload that exfiltrates network probe results
details: Package self-describes as 'A simple date formatting utility' but its postinstall.js runs an internal-network reconnaissance payload at npm install time. On install, the script executes nslookup/curl/ping against ByteDance-internal hosts (e.g. src-ssrf.bytedance.net, api.coze.cn, integration.coze.cn) and iterates SSRF-bypass payloads, then POSTs every probe result via curl to http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com — a public out-of-band interaction service controlled by a third party. The destination (BURL) and target host are hardcoded at postinstall.js:8 and:24. Behavior runs automatically via the postinstall lifecycle hook with no consent, no opt-out, and no disclosure in the package's stated purpose. Any installer that is not the intended internal target has their network reachability information leaked to an attacker-controlled OOB endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
